mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.5 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving equity earnings revisions upward and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in housing starts and nonfarm payrolls confirms broad-based economic expansion, validating corporate revenue forecasts and triggering positive S&P 500 forward returns over a six-week horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 55 percent of out-of-sample windows, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During supply-constrained late-cycle expansions, a sustained rise in the industrial production index signals capacity bottlenecks that compress profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth correlates positively with SPX 5-day returns in more than 65 percent of observations during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly real GDP level exceeding 13500 billion dollars indicates an overheating economy that forces the Federal Reserve to accelerate policy normalization, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns remain positive in more than 60 percent of quarters where real GDP surpasses the 13500 billion dollar threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real consumer credit balances combined with accelerating consumer price inflation erode household purchasing power and trigger margin compression for consumer-facing equities, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a six-week horizon.
failure_condition: The combined credit and inflation signal predicts negative 60-day returns in fewer than 40 percent of out-of-sample periods, or the joint coefficient loses statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: A consistent month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payrolls reflects durable economic momentum that supports corporate earnings trajectories, mechanically driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: Payroll growth correlates negatively with SPX 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of rolling 12-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: In an aggressive monetary tightening cycle, a rising federal funds rate paired with accelerating consumer price inflation compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and margin squeeze, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint rate and inflation signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of tightening episodes, or the interaction term becomes statistically insignificant.
